# Distribution Host Integration Contracts

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/hostintegrationdoc -write-doc. DO NOT EDIT. -->

> Riido task: RIID-4670 [Contracts] distribution/provider routing status contract migration

Executable SSOT: `github.com/teamswyg/riido-contracts/hostintegration` plus `docs/20-domain/distribution-host-integration.riido.json`.

The hostintegration package owns public distribution-channel and provider-routing vocabulary only; runtime host behavior stays downstream.

- Evidence artifact: `host-integration-evidence`
- Distribution valid: `true`; provider routing valid: `true`
- Store-managed classification exclusive: `true`

## Distribution channels

- `developer-id`
- `mac-app-store`
- `msix-sideload`
- `msix-store`
- `dev-local`

## Store-managed channels

- `mac-app-store`
- `msix-store`

## Provider routing statuses

- `available`
- `login-required`
- `unsupported`
- `store-blocked`

## Non-owned surfaces

- `app data root selection`
- `local IPC`
- `workspace grants`
- `consent ledgers`
- `review/demo mode`
- `privacy metadata allowlist artifacts`
- `provider executable discovery`
- `provider login probes`
- `provider process execution`
- `control-plane HTTP handlers`
- `Terraform, AWS accounts, or deployment evidence`

## Invariant anchors

- `DistributionChannel.Valid is the executable gate for distribution channel vocabulary`
- `DistributionChannel.StoreManaged is true only for app-store managed channels`
- `ProviderRoutingStatus.Valid is the executable gate for server-facing provider routing status`
- `hostintegration never exposes provider paths, provider tokens, API keys, or raw environment values`

## Evidence Loop

- Observe: The hostintegration package already contained executable vocabulary, but the public reader was still hand-maintained prose.
- Hypothesis: A manifest-backed verifier can prove the reader, channel/status constants, store-managed classification, and boundary list stay aligned.
- Execute: Read the hostintegration package functions, verify counts and validity methods, regenerate the reader, and publish evidence from CI.
- Evaluate: CI fails when distribution channels, store-managed channels, provider routing statuses, boundary counts, or the generated reader drift.
- Retrospective: Future host integration vocabulary should extend the package and manifest first, then regenerate the reader instead of editing prose.
